institution: Sector-wide typology - Indian retail digital payments
source_documents: https://www.npci.org.in (UPI circulars - collect-request limits, fraud advisories)
category: Digital payment fraud - QR code scan-to-pay deception
risk_signals: Victim is told to scan a QR code 'to receive' a payment or refund
A marketplace buyer/seller insists the counterparty scan a QR to complete a deal
QR shared over chat/email from an unverified source
analyst_reasoning: Scanning a UPI QR always initiates a payment FROM the scanner. Fraudsters reframe a scan as a way to receive money, so the victim authorises a debit believing it is a credit. Like the collect-request scam, the deception inverts the direction of money flow.
investigation_steps: Trace the QR's encoded VPA and amount and match it to the debit in the victim's log
Assess whether the payee VPA is newly created or linked to prior complaints
Establish the pretext channel (classifieds, social media, chat)
decision: Freeze/blacklist the payee VPA, educate the customer that scanning a QR only ever pays out, and file a prompt fraud report.
outcome: The scanned amount is debited to the fraudster; recovery hinges on early reporting and mule-account freezing.
lessons_learned: Scanning any payment QR sends money out; it never receives money
A counterparty who insists you scan a QR to 'get paid' is a strong fraud signal
Consumer education on scan-direction is the cheapest control
